steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to contrast two chemical compounds: dichloromethane (an HCC, containing hydrogen, chlorine, and carbon) and tetradecafluorohexane (a PFC, containing fluorine and carbon). The answer must highlight what's different between them — specifically their elemental compositions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'presents both compounds' compositions in parallel form': Lays out each compound's elements side by side, making the elemental difference immediately visible. ✓
  • Choice B — 'emphasizes what the compounds share': Stresses similarities (both contain carbon, both are synthetic), which is the opposite of a contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — 'mentions only one compound': Can't create a contrast between two things if only one is described. ✗
  • Choice D — 'focuses on the compounds' uses rather than their compositions': Shifts away from elemental makeup, so the elemental contrast is lost. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
